steps.title: Use roots to test possible factors
steps.content: A quick Desmos method is to test each answer choice's zero. If an expression is a factor, its zero should make the original polynomial equal $0$.

steps.title: Match the zero to the answer choice
steps.content: The tested roots come from the choices: A has root $12$, B has root $-6$, C has root $-1$, and D has root $6$. Desmos shows $P(-6)=0$, so the factor with root $-6$ is the one that works.

steps.title: Confirm the answer
steps.content: The expression with root $-6$ is $2x+12$, which is choice B. Therefore choice B is correct.
